unmapped.human_action: Manually assess whether OTM call spreads on LITE or COHR at 20-25% OTM and 50-79 DTE present exploitable asymmetry after post-announcement IV expansion; the scanner's current filter configuration may be excluding structures that exist. Date the next discrete catalyst: Lumentum Q4 FY2026 earnings and the first explicit NVIDIA purchase-commitment burn disclosure in a quarterly filing.

unmapped.machine_action: Expand scanner watchlist to include LITE, COHR, AAOI, MACOM, POET, nLight for next scan cycle. Add NVIDIA commitment burn cadence as a monitored disclosure field in the photonic-computing decision packet. Flag next OFC trade-show equivalent as a design-win disclosure event.
unmapped.methodology_footer: Corpus claims sourced from primary IR disclosures: Lumentum Q2 and Q3 FY2026 earnings releases, Coherent Q3 FY2026 earnings release, NVIDIA newsroom release dated 2026-03-02. Private marks sourced from company fundraise announcements and corroborating investor communications. Scanner results reflect 2026-04-28 scan; LITE and COHR returned zero passing contracts under current filter configuration across all scanned expiries.
unmapped.position_conflict_disclosure: No current book or paper exposure in photonic-computing sector names. No LITE, COHR, AAOI, MACOM, MRVL, or directly related positions appear in the open or recently closed trade log. No conflict with existing cicadas-theme ag/FX book.
unmapped.uncertainty: The scanner finding zero contracts for LITE/COHR is genuinely ambiguous: it may reflect implied volatility that has already taxed the asymmetric call structure away following the post-NVIDIA-announcement equity move, or it may reflect scanner filter gaps at the current strike/expiry configuration. The 'largest ever' framing for the NVIDIA commitment is not independently provable across all prior internal hyperscaler or foundry programs and is not repeated in this brief. Private chiplet milestones (Lightmatter, Ayar, Salience) derive from a single source category — company and investor announcements — and should be treated as directional rather than independently audited.

signals: Lumentum fiscal Q3 2026 revenue $808.4M, up 90% year over year; OCS backlog baseline exceeded $400M as of Q2
Coherent fiscal Q3 2026 revenue $1.806B, up approximately 21% year over year
NVIDIA March 2 2026 $4B strategic commitment: $2B equity investment each to LITE and COHR plus multibillion purchase commitments and future capacity rights
Lightmatter $850M total raised at $4.4B valuation; Passage L200/L200x above 200 Tbps total bandwidth, 8x training-time claim for advanced AI models
Ayar Labs $870M total raised after $500M Series E in March 2026 at $3.75B valuation; targeting high-volume manufacturing by 2028
Marvell acquired Celestial AI February 2026; guiding $500M ARR in Q4 FY2028 from Celestial Photonic Fabric revenue stream
Scanner finds zero qualifying options contracts for LITE or COHR under current filter configuration
Convergence score for LITE sits at 0.4 despite three independent corpus claims rated 0.85-0.88 confidence

The 'largest ever' framing for the NVIDIA commitment is not independently provable across all prior internal hyperscaler or foundry programs and is not repeated in this brief. title: The Bandwidth Wall Is Already Photons
summary: Optical interconnect has crossed from research to production revenue inside the hyperscaler AI buildout, anchored by NVIDIA's March 2026 $4B dual commitment to Lumentum and Coherent, yet the options scanner finds zero qualifying structures for either name while private chiplet marks at Lightmatter ($4.4B) and Ayar Labs ($3.75B) signal the same 12-to-24-month private-leads-public revaluation pattern visible across five other sectors in this corpus.
